channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: America’s data-centre backlash puts the AI boom at risk
item.description: Opposition is spreading across the country

item.title: U.S., Iran Rally Support for Interim Peace Deal Abroad
item.description: But competing assertions have Gulf nations worried that the agreement won’t last.

item.title: Why the World Cup Keeps Eluding China
item.description: Xi has spent billions of dollars trying to turn Beijing into a soccer powerhouse.

item.title: Waiving sanctions on Iranian oil is a huge concession by America
item.description: It will enrich an adversary

item.title: Why macro trading is hard
item.description: More difficult than knowing what to buy is knowing how much

item.title: Russia’s Crimean conquest is turning into a deadly mess
item.description: Ukrainian drones have made the resort region a battlezone

item.title: Child care is becoming more affordable
item.description: Our ranking compares nursery bills to income in 36 rich countries

item.title: Loyalty Without Leverage: India’s US Tilt Yields Little
item.description: India’s attempt to balance ties with the U.S. has turned into a tilt, still deemed insufficient by Washington. For India, it is all stick and no carrots.

item.title: How the Quad Can Deliver in a New Strategic Era
item.description: The regular tempo of on-the-ground cooperation suggests that the Quad – despite its thin institutional structure – may in fact be insulated from higher level political volatility.

item.title: How China’s ‘Red Lines’ Are Quietly Shaping Global News Reporting
item.description: Under Xi Jinping, the CCP has expanded its control over political language to the point where it challenges journalism’s most basic task: describing the world accurately.

item.title: The Microfinance Debate Is Missing the Point
item.description: For too long, policymakers, economists, and investors have focused on whether microfinance actually helps people, reducing a complex issue to a meaningless yes-or-no verdict. The most important questions concern how loans are designed, delivered, and regulated, who receives them, and what they are used for.

item.title: Poland, Ukraine, and the Ongoing Battle Over the Ukrainian Insurgent Army
item.description: An ugly chapter of World War II history is straining relations between Warsaw and Kyiv.

item.title: Japan Is Investing in Offshore Wind in the UK. What About at Home?
item.description: The Japan-U.K. compact shows Japanese companies still have an appetite for offshore wind, but local communities must not be left behind.

item.title: Turning Latin America’s Resilience Into Growth
item.description: Economic stabilization has been a significant achievement for Latin America and the Caribbean, a region with a long history of financial crises. But these countries need a well-sequenced reform agenda to attract private investment and create the growth engine that will translate hard-won gains into higher living standards.

item.title: The Structural Limits of the EU’s China Policy
item.description: The European Council’s latest mandate pairs dialogue with prospective new trade defense tools, institutionalizing an approach that complicates long-term ties with Beijing.

item.title: An Australian View of the New Trump Iran Deal
item.description: Australia’s geography may provide it with some insulation from conflict, but its maritime dependency creates other vulnerabilities.

item.title: The U.S. Won the War With Iran
item.description: It wasn’t a knockout blow, but Washington prevailed on points.

item.title: Is the MOU a Treaty or Not? And Why Should We Care?
item.description: Confusing language in the U.S.-Iran cease-fire agreement makes its legal status unclear.

item.title: The World Cup vs. the Extremists
item.description: Germany’s 2026 World Cup team, like so many others in the tournament, shows that loving one’s country and welcoming newcomers are not incompatible impulses. Although activists on the far left and the far right would have us believe otherwise, most ordinary people implicitly recognize this.

item.title: Smartphones and AI are remaking rural India
item.description: Villages have fallen in love with short-form videos and chatbots

item.title: Beyond Brexit—and Back to Europe
item.description: Those who follow only intergovernmental negotiations or debates in the media might think that nothing has changed in the decade since the United Kingdom's voters decided to leave the European Union. Yet new polling finds that large majorities on both sides of the English Channel now support re-engagement.

item.title: How America Made the Mafia
item.description: Organized crime and U.S. global influence in the 20th century.

item.title: From Bloody Kansas to the Rebuilding of Europe
item.description: In trying to restore order after World War II, U.S. leaders drew lessons from a dark chapter in their country’s history.

item.title: What Happened to Indonesia’s Booming Tech Sector?
item.description: Despite their initial promise, many of the country's major tech firms are struggling to create value for shareholders.

item.title: Why the China-Iran Relationship Has Been Friendly But Distant Since Ancient Times
item.description: The two civilizations have consistently found each other to be useful, yet they haven’t invested heavily in their partnership.

item.title: Why “China First” Will Fail
item.description: The limits and lessons of a transactional foreign policy.

item.title: Losing the War of the Future
item.description: How new technologies threaten America’s military advantage.

item.title: The Broken Nuclear Umbrella
item.description: What comes after extended deterrence.

item.title: The Afghanistan Reckoning
item.description: Forever wars and the costs of collective forgetting.

item.title: How to Survive the AI Shock
item.description: A policy playbook to avert political crisis.

item.title: Heartland vs. Rimland
item.description: The battle lines in the war for the next global order.

item.title: The Mirage of China's Military Edge
item.description: Panic is misguided—and counterproductive.

item.title: The Next Russia Threat
item.description: Moscow’s military power after Ukraine.

item.title: Bangladeshi PM Tarique Rahman Asks Malaysia to Lift Restrictions on Labor Migration
item.description: Kuala Lumpur has recently tightened the intake of Bangladeshi workers due to concerns about abuse, debt bondage, and excessive recruitment fees.

item.title: Uyghurs Wonder: Does the US Still Care About Human Rights in China?
item.description: After a disappointing Trump-Xi summit, Uyghurs in the United States are divided on Washington’s commitment to ending abuses in China.

item.title: How to turn computing power into a financial asset
item.description: Entrepreneurs, exchange operators and AI firms are creating tradable instruments backed by processing power
